'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Astronomy Master|ja|天文博士}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} '''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030) *Children by unknown mothers: '''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}
== Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1990.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Astronomy Master|ja|天文博士}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} '''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030) *Children by unknown mothers: '''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Astronomy Master|ja|天文博士}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} '''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030) *Children by unknown mothers: '''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}
== Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}

revision.text:
'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Astronomy Master|ja|天文博士}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==

He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} '''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030) *Children by unknown mothers: '''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}

revision.text:
'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Astronomy Master|ja|天文博士}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} '''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030) *Children by unknown mothers: '''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}
== Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1990.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Astronomy Master|ja|天文博士}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} '''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030) *Children by unknown mothers: '''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}
== Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1990.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''

== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.

This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.

* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''

Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''

His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.

* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}

revision.text:
'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.

* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''

revision.text:
'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1990.

Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
