revision.text:
'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1990.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1990.

He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==

In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>

Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>

In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.

* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1990.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1990.

revision.text:
'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1990.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "大炊寮}}" to "大炊寮|1=Ōiryō}}"
revision.text:
'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1990.

revision.text:
'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing the Calendar Path to Mitsuyoshi and the {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Jushii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} (Senior Fourth Rank) and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Calendar Master|ja|暦博士}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Calendar Master and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|暦博士}}" to "|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}"
revision.text:
'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1990.

Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.

* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇|12=draft}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

revision.text:
'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族|12=draft}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|12=draft}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|12=draft}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲|12=draft}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|12=draft}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|12=draft}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|12=draft}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō|12=draft}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|12=draft}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|12=draft}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇|12=draft}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=draft}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|12=draft}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=draft}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=draft}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明|12=draft}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=draft}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇|12=draft}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲|12=draft}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣|12=draft}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道|12=draft}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行|12=draft}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆|12=draft}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師|12=draft}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京|12=draft}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|12=draft}}, 1990.

Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} at the age of 77.
