'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|12=draft|12=draft}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲|12=draft|12=draft}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|12=draft|12=draft}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft|12=draft}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|12=draft|12=draft}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|12=draft|12=draft}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|12=draft|12=draft}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|12=draft|12=draft}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft|12=draft}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=draft|12=draft}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|12=draft|12=draft}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft|12=draft}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=draft|12=draft}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=draft|12=draft}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明|12=draft|12=draft}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=draft|12=draft}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} at the age of 77.

His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft|12=draft}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=draft|12=draft}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=draft|12=draft}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明|12=draft|12=draft}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=draft|12=draft}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} at the age of 77.

* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|12=draft|12=draft}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲|12=draft|12=draft}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|12=draft|12=draft}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft|12=draft}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|12=draft|12=draft}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|12=draft|12=draft}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|12=draft|12=draft}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|12=draft|12=draft}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft|12=draft}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=draft|12=draft}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|12=draft|12=draft}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft|12=draft}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=draft|12=draft}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=draft|12=draft}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明|12=draft|12=draft}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=draft|12=draft}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} at the age of 77.

It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft|12=draft}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=draft|12=draft}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|12=draft|12=draft}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft|12=draft}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=draft|12=draft}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=draft|12=draft}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明|12=draft|12=draft}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=draft|12=draft}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|12=draft|12=draft}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲|12=draft|12=draft}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|12=draft|12=draft}}. He held the rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft|12=draft}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|12=draft|12=draft}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|12=draft|12=draft}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|12=draft|12=draft}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|12=draft|12=draft}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft|12=draft}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=draft|12=draft}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>

His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft|12=draft}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=draft|12=draft}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=draft|12=draft}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明|12=draft|12=draft}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=draft|12=draft}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} at the age of 77.

Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|12=draft|12=draft}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō|2=ja|3=大炊寮|lt=Bureau of Palace Kitchens|1=Ōiryō|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|12=draft|12=draft}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|12=draft|12=draft}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft|12=draft}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=draft|12=draft}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|12=draft|12=draft}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|12=draft|12=draft}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=draft|12=draft}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=draft|12=draft}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明|12=draft|12=draft}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=draft|12=draft}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} at the age of 77.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō|2=ja|3=大炊寮|lt=Bureau of Palace Kitchens|1=Ōiryō}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''

revision.comment: 37 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:賀茂光栄 up to rev 99043590
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 賀茂 光栄 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[平安時代]]中期 | 生誕 = [[天慶]]2年（[[939年]]） | 死没 = [[長和]]4年[[6月7日 (旧暦)|6月7日]]（[[1015年]][[6月25日]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[従四位|従四位上]]・[[京職|右京権大夫]] | 主君 = [[村上天皇]]→[[冷泉天皇]]→[[円融天皇]]→[[花山天皇]]→[[一条天皇]] | 氏族 = [[賀茂朝臣氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[賀茂保憲]] | 兄弟 = '''光栄'''、光国、光輔、女子 | 妻 = 弓削仲宣娘 | 子 = [[賀茂守道|守道]]、[[賀茂義行|義行]] | 特記事項 = }} '''賀茂 光栄'''（かも の みつよし）は、[[平安時代]]中期の[[貴族]]・[[陰陽家]]。[[丹波国#国司|丹波権介]]・[[賀茂忠行]]の孫で、[[陰陽寮|陰陽頭]]・[[賀茂保憲]]の長男。[[官位]]は[[従四位|従四位上]]・[[京職|右京権大夫]]。
== 経歴 == [[暦博士]]・[[天文博士]]・[[大炊寮|大炊頭]]・[[主計寮|主計頭]]などを歴任、一説には陰陽允を歴任したというが可能性は低い。[[一条天皇|一条朝]]初頭の[[長徳]]4年（[[998年]]）[[大炊寮|大炊権頭]]から大炊頭に昇任されるが、職務に堪えられるかどうかはわからないが、一道（[[陰陽道]]）の長であることを以て任ぜられたという。[[長保]]2年（[[1000年]]）弟の前[[内蔵寮|内蔵允]]・賀茂光国に[[暦道]]を習伝させるよう命ぜられている<ref>『権記』長保2年7月9日条</ref>。
祖父・父に同じく抜群の腕を持つ[[陰陽師]]であったという。父・保憲は家学であった[[陰陽道]]のうち[[暦道]]を光栄に、[[天文道]]を愛弟子の[[安倍晴明]]に譲り、陰陽道の二大宗家「[[安賀両家]]」が成立した。光栄はなぜ賀茂氏の家学である陰陽道を分割してまで[[阿倍氏|安倍氏]]に宗家の地位を与えたのか疑問に思っていたらしく、晴明と争論したという<ref>『[[続古事談]]』</ref>。
[[三条天皇|三条朝]]の[[長和]]4年（[[1015年]]）6月7日[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]。[[享年]]77。
== 官歴 == *時期不詳：[[従五位|従五位上]]<ref name="eu" /> *[[寛和]]2年（[[986年]]） 11月1日：見[[暦博士]]兼[[備中国#国司|備中介]]<ref name="eu">『九条家本延喜式裏暦』</ref> *時期不詳：[[正五位|正五位下]]<ref name="mkkc" /> *[[長徳]]3年（[[997年]]） 11月11日：[[大炊寮|大炊権頭]]兼播磨権介<ref name="mkkc">『御堂関白記』長徳4年具注暦</ref> *長徳4年（[[998年]]） 8月27日：大炊頭<ref name="gk">『権記』</ref> *[[長和]]2年（[[1013年]]） 正月10日：見[[京職|右京権大夫]]<ref name="syk">『小右記』</ref> *長和4年（[[1015年]]） 6月7日：[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]<ref name="syk" />
== 系譜 == *父：[[賀茂保憲]] *母：不詳 *妻：弓削仲宣の娘 **男子：[[賀茂守道]]（986-1030） *生母不明の子女 **男子：[[賀茂義行]]
== 登場する創作作品 == *[[久遠の絆]] *[[陰陽師 (漫画)|陰陽師]] *[[陰陽ノ京]] *平安あかしあやかし陰陽師 *[[ねこねこ日本史]] *京都伏見のあやかし甘味帖
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *宮崎康充編『国司補任 第四』[[続群書類従完成会]]、1990年

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 賀茂 光栄 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[平安時代]]中期 | 生誕 = [[天慶]]2年（[[939年]]） | 死没 = [[長和]]4年[[6月7日 (旧暦)|6月7日]]（[[1015年]][[6月25日]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[従四位|従四位上]]・[[京職|右京権大夫]] | 主君 = [[村上天皇]]→[[冷泉天皇]]→[[円融天皇]]→[[花山天皇]]→[[一条天皇]] | 氏族 = [[賀茂朝臣氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[賀茂保憲]] | 兄弟 = '''光栄'''、光国、光輔、女子 | 妻 = 弓削仲宣娘 | 子 = [[賀茂守道|守道]]、[[賀茂義行|義行]] | 特記事項 = }} '''賀茂 光栄'''（かも の みつよし）は、[[平安時代]]中期の[[貴族]]・[[陰陽家]]。[[丹波国#国司|丹波権介]]・[[賀茂忠行]]の孫で、[[陰陽寮|陰陽頭]]・[[賀茂保憲]]の長男。[[官位]]は[[従四位|従四位上]]・[[京職|右京権大夫]]。
== 経歴 == [[暦博士]]・[[天文博士]]・[[大炊寮|大炊頭]]・[[主計寮|主計頭]]などを歴任、一説には陰陽允を歴任したというが可能性は低い。[[一条天皇|一条朝]]初頭の[[長徳]]4年（[[998年]]）[[大炊寮|大炊権頭]]から大炊頭に昇任されるが、職務に堪えられるかどうかはわからないが、一道（[[陰陽道]]）の長であることを以て任ぜられたという。[[長保]]2年（[[1000年]]）弟の前[[内蔵寮|内蔵允]]・賀茂光国に[[暦道]]を習伝させるよう命ぜられている<ref>『権記』長保2年7月9日条</ref>。
祖父・父に同じく抜群の腕を持つ[[陰陽師]]であったという。父・保憲は家学であった[[陰陽道]]のうち[[暦道]]を光栄に、[[天文道]]を愛弟子の[[安倍晴明]]に譲り、陰陽道の二大宗家「[[安賀両家]]」が成立した。光栄はなぜ賀茂氏の家学である陰陽道を分割してまで[[阿倍氏|安倍氏]]に宗家の地位を与えたのか疑問に思っていたらしく、晴明と争論したという<ref>『[[続古事談]]』</ref>。
[[三条天皇|三条朝]]の[[長和]]4年（[[1015年]]）6月7日[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]。[[享年]]77。
== 官歴 == *時期不詳：[[従五位|従五位上]]<ref name="eu" /> *[[寛和]]2年（[[986年]]） 11月1日：見[[暦博士]]兼[[備中国#国司|備中介]]<ref name="eu">『九条家本延喜式裏暦』</ref> *時期不詳：[[正五位|正五位下]]<ref name="mkkc" /> *[[長徳]]3年（[[997年]]） 11月11日：[[大炊寮|大炊権頭]]兼播磨権介<ref name="mkkc">『御堂関白記』長徳4年具注暦</ref> *長徳4年（[[998年]]） 8月27日：大炊頭<ref name="gk">『権記』</ref> *[[長和]]2年（[[1013年]]） 正月10日：見[[京職|右京権大夫]]<ref name="syk">『小右記』</ref> *長和4年（[[1015年]]） 6月7日：[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]<ref name="syk" />
== 系譜 == *父：[[賀茂保憲]] *母：不詳 *妻：弓削仲宣の娘 **男子：[[賀茂守道]]（986-1030） *生母不明の子女 **男子：[[賀茂義行]]
== 登場する創作作品 == *[[久遠の絆]] *[[陰陽師 (漫画)|陰陽師]] *[[陰陽ノ京]] *平安あかしあやかし陰陽師 *[[ねこねこ日本史]] *京都伏見のあやかし甘味帖
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *宮崎康充編『国司補任 第四』[[続群書類従完成会]]、1990年
{{DEFAULTSORT:かも の みつよし}} [[Category:賀茂朝臣氏|みつよし]] [[Category:平安時代中期の貴族]] [[Category:陰陽師]] [[Category:従四位上受位者]] [[Category:939年生]] [[Category:1015年没]]

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō|2=ja|3=大炊寮|lt=Bureau of Palace Kitchens|1=Ōiryō}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会}}, 1990.

revision.text:
'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|noble|ja|貴族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/noble|qq=}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|qq=}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|qq=}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Yasunori|qq=}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|qq=}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōkura Bureau|ja|大炊寮|1=Ōiryō|2=ja|3=大炊寮|lt=Bureau of Palace Kitchens|1=Ōiryō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkura Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiryō|qq=}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|qq=}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|qq=}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|ja|一条天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ichijō|qq=}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|qq=}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|ja|陰陽道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōdō|qq=}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Seimei|qq=}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|qq=}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|ja|三条天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sanjō|qq=}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo no Yasunori|qq=}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Nakatsuna|qq=}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Morimichi|qq=}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Yoshiyuki|qq=}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwano no Kizuna|qq=}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji (manga)|ja|陰陽師|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji (manga)|qq=}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kyō|qq=}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|qq=}}, 1990.
{{translated page|ja|賀茂光栄|version=99043590|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|qq=}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|qq=}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).

He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|qq=}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. revision.text:
'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|qq=}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|qq=}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the [[:en:Ōiryō|Bureau of Palace Kitchens]] (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|qq=}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|qq=}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|qq=}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Nakatsuna|qq=}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Morimichi|qq=}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Yoshiyuki|qq=}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwano no Kizuna|qq=}}'' * ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kyō|qq=}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|qq=}}, 1990.
{{translated page|ja|賀茂光栄|version=99043590|comment=Imported full ja history}}

He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|qq=}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|qq=}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|qq=}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|qq=}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the [[:en:Ōiryō|Bureau of Palace Kitchens]] (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|qq=}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:従四位上受位者]] into [[Category:Recipients of Upper Junior Fourth Rank]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|qq=}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|qq=}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the [[:en:Ōiryō|Bureau of Palace Kitchens]] (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|qq=}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Nakatsuna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Morimichi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Yoshiyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwano no Kizuna|qq=}}'' * ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kyō|qq=}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''

* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwano no Kizuna|qq=}}'' * ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kyō|qq=}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|qq=}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|qq=}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the [[:en:Ōiryō|Bureau of Palace Kitchens]] (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|qq=}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Nakatsuna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Morimichi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Yoshiyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwano no Kizuna|qq=}}'' * ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kyō|qq=}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|qq=}}, 1990.
{{translated page|ja|賀茂光栄|version=99043590|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--> [[Category:Aristocrats of the Mid-Heian Period]] [[Category:Recipients of Upper Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{Infobox person|birth_date=0939|death_date={{death date|1015|6|25}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|qq=|1=Kamo no Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634938}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|qq=|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374719}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the [[:en:Ōiryō|Bureau of Palace Kitchens]] (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|qq=|1=Kamo and Abe Clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451490}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.

* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|qq=|1=Kamo and Abe Clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451490}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Nakatsuna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Morimichi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Yoshiyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwano no Kizuna|qq=|1=Kuwano no Kizuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10877708}}'' * ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kyō|qq=|1=Onmyō no Kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658258}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''

revision.text:
{{Infobox person|birth_date=0939|death_date={{death date|1015|6|25}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|qq=|1=Kamo no Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634938}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|qq=|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374719}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the [[:en:Ōiryō|Bureau of Palace Kitchens]] (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|qq=|1=Kamo and Abe Clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451490}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Nakatsuna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Morimichi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Yoshiyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwano no Kizuna|qq=|1=Kuwano no Kizuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10877708}}'' * ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kyō|qq=|1=Onmyō no Kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658258}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''

{{Infobox person|birth_date=0939|death_date={{death date|1015|6|25}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|qq=|1=Kamo no Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634938}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|qq=|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374719}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the [[:en:Ōiryō|Bureau of Palace Kitchens]] (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|qq=|1=Kamo and Abe Clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451490}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Nakatsuna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Morimichi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Yoshiyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwano no Kizuna|qq=|1=Kuwano no Kizuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10877708}}'' * ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kyō|qq=|1=Onmyō no Kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658258}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
