<!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--> [[Category:Aristocrats of the Mid-Heian Period]] [[Category:Recipients of Upper Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11634934}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0939|death_date={{death date|1015|6|25}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|qq=|1=Kamo no Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634938}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|qq=|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374719}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the [[:en:Ōiryō|Bureau of Palace Kitchens]] (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|qq=|1=Kamo and Abe Clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451490}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==

*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Yoshiyuki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0939|death_date={{death date|1015|6|25}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|1=Kamo no Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634938}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374719}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the [[:en:Ōiryō|Bureau of Palace Kitchens]] (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|1=Kamo and Abe Clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451490}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|1=Kamo and Abe Clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451490}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

*Father: [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Nakatsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Morimichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0939|death_date={{death date|1015|6|25}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|1=Kamo no Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634938}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374719}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the [[:en:Ōiryō|Bureau of Palace Kitchens]] (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|1=Kamo and Abe Clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451490}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Nakatsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Morimichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==

Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the [[:en:Ōiryō|Bureau of Palace Kitchens]] (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|1=Kamo and Abe Clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451490}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0939|death_date={{death date|1015|6|25}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|1=Kamo no Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634938}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374719}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).

Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the [[:en:Ōiryō|Bureau of Palace Kitchens]] (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}. In the early reign of [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|1=Kamo and Abe Clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451490}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0939|death_date={{death date|1015|6|25}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|1=Kamo no Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634938}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374719}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the [[:en:Ōiryō|Bureau of Palace Kitchens]] (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|1=Kamo and Abe Clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451490}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0939|death_date={{death date|1015|6|25}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|1=Kamo no Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634938}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374719}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the [[:en:Ōiryō|Bureau of Palace Kitchens]] (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|1=Kamo and Abe Clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451490}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Nakatsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Morimichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwano no Kizuna|1=Kuwano no Kizuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10877708}}'' * ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kyō|1=Onmyō no Kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658258}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|1=zokugunshoruijuukanseikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686030}}, 1990.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0939|death_date={{death date|1015|6|25}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|1=Kamo no Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634938}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374719}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the [[:en:Ōiryō|Bureau of Palace Kitchens]] (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|1=Kamo and Abe Clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451490}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Nakatsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Morimichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwano no Kizuna|1=Kuwano no Kizuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10877708}}'' * ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kyō|1=Onmyō no Kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658258}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0939|death_date={{death date|1015|6|25}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|1=Kamo no Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634938}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374719}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the [[:en:Ōiryō|Bureau of Palace Kitchens]] (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]] rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]]. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]], passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]]. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|1=Kamo and Abe Clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451490}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sanjō|Emperor Sanjō]] at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.

* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Nakatsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Morimichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwano no Kizuna|1=Kuwano no Kizuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10877708}}'' * ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kyō|1=Onmyō no Kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658258}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|1=zokugunshoruijuukanseikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686030}}, 1990.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0939|death_date={{death date|1015|6|25}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11634934]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|WD=Q134737|lt=noble}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|1=Kamo no Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634938}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374719}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōiryō|en|Ōiryō|WD=Q8081513|lt=Bureau of Palace Kitchens}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|en|Emperor Ichijō|WD=Q449540|lt=Emperor Ichijō}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|1=Kamo and Abe Clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451490}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|en|Emperor Sanjō|WD=Q357231|lt=Emperor Sanjō}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Nakatsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Morimichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwano no Kizuna|1=Kuwano no Kizuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10877708}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji (manga)}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kyō|1=Onmyō no Kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658258}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|1=zokugunshoruijuukanseikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686030}}, 1990.

Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōiryō|en|Ōiryō|WD=Q8081513|lt=Bureau of Palace Kitchens}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|en|Emperor Ichijō|WD=Q449540|lt=Emperor Ichijō}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|1=Kamo and Abe Clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451490}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|en|Emperor Sanjō|WD=Q357231|lt=Emperor Sanjō}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Nakatsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Morimichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwano no Kizuna|1=Kuwano no Kizuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10877708}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji (manga)}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kyō|1=Onmyō no Kyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658258}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==
* Miyazaki Yasumitsu (ed.), ''Kokushi Hōnin Vol. 4,'' {{ill|Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|ja|続群書類従完成会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokugunshoruijūkansei-kai|1=zokugunshoruijuukanseikai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686030}}, 1990.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0939|death_date={{death date|1015|6|25}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}
'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|WD=Q134737|lt=noble}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|1=Kamo no Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634938}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374719}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōiryō|en|Ōiryō|WD=Q8081513|lt=Bureau of Palace Kitchens}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|en|Emperor Ichijō|WD=Q449540|lt=Emperor Ichijō}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|1=Kamo and Abe Clans|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451490}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|en|Emperor Sanjō|WD=Q357231|lt=Emperor Sanjō}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==

'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|WD=Q134737|lt=noble|1=Nobility}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji|1=Onmyōji}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|1=Kamo no Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634938}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori|1=Kamo no Yasunori}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374719}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōiryō|en|Ōiryō|WD=Q8081513|lt=Bureau of Palace Kitchens|1=Ōiryō}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|en|Emperor Ichijō|WD=Q449540|lt=Emperor Ichijō|1=Emperor Ichijō}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji|1=Onmyōji}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|1=Q11451490|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11451490}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|en|Emperor Sanjō|WD=Q357231|lt=Emperor Sanjō|1=Emperor Sanjō}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori|1=Kamo no Yasunori}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Nakatsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Morimichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwano no Kizuna|1=Kuon no Kizuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10877708}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji (manga)|1=Onmyōji}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kyō|1=Q11658258|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658258}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References ==

<!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--><!--Kamo no Mitsuyoshi--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11634934}} [[Category:1015 deaths]] [[Category:939 births]] [[Category:Aristocrats of the Mid-Heian Period]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:Kamo no Asomi Clan]] [[Category:Onmyōji]] [[Category:Recipients of Upper Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:賀茂光栄]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0939|death_date={{death date|1015|6|25}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}}}
'''Kamo Mitsuyoshi''' (賀茂 光栄) was a mid-Heian period {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|qid=Q134737|lt=noble|1=Nobility}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|qid=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji|1=Onmyōji}}. He was the grandson of {{ill|Kamo Tadayuki|ja|賀茂忠行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Tadayuki|1=Kamo no Tadayuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11634938}}, the Tanba Gon-no-suke (Provisional Assistant Governor of Tanba Province), and the eldest son of {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|qid=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori|1=Kamo no Yasunori}}, the head of the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} and the position of {{ill|Kyō no Geki|ja|京職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyō no Geki|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11374719}} (Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital).
== Biography ==
Mitsuyoshi held various positions, including {{ill|Reki Hakase|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase=Tenmon Hakase|lt=Astronomy Master|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, Head of the {{ill|Ōiryō|en|Ōiryō|qid=Q8081513|lt=Bureau of Palace Kitchens|1=Ōiryō}} (Ōi no Kami), and {{ill|Master of Accounts|ja|主計頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Master of Accounts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11368792}}. He is also said to have served as an {{ill|Onmyōji|ja|陰陽允|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, although this is considered unlikely. In the early reign of {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|en|Emperor Ichijō|qid=Q449540|lt=Emperor Ichijō|1=Emperor Ichijō}}, he was promoted from Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau to its full headship in 998 (Chōtoku 4). It was said that this promotion reflected his leadership in the {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|qid=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}} rather than his administrative capabilities. In 1000 (Chōhō 2), he was instructed to teach the Calendar Path ({{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}}) to his younger brother, Kamo Mitsukuni.<ref>''Gonki,'' Chōhō 2, 7th month, 9th day.</ref>
Like his father and grandfather, Mitsuyoshi was a distinguished {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|qid=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji|1=Onmyōji}}. His father, Yasunori, split the hereditary traditions of the {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|qid=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}}, passing down {{ill|Rekidō|ja|暦道|1=Rekidō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|13=User:Immanuelle/Rekidō|qq=}} to Mitsuyoshi and {{ill|Astronomy Path|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy Path|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} to his apprentice, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|qid=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}}. This division resulted in the formation of the two great houses of Onmyōdō, known as the {{ill|Kamo and Abe Clans|ja|安賀両家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo and Abe Clans|1=Q11451490|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11451490}}. Mitsuyoshi is said to have questioned why Yasunori allowed the Abe Clan to share the family’s religious authority, leading to disputes with Seimei.<ref>''Zoku Kojidan'' (続古事談).</ref>
Mitsuyoshi passed away on the 7th day of the 6th month in 1015 (Chōwa 4) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sanjō|en|Emperor Sanjō|qid=Q357231|lt=Emperor Sanjō|1=Emperor Sanjō}} at the age of 77.
== Official Positions ==
* Undated: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade<ref name="eu" /> * 986 (Kanna 2): Reki Hakase and Provisional Assistant Governor of Bitchū Province<ref name="eu">''Kujō-ke Hon Engishiki Urarekisho''.</ref> * Undated: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade<ref name="mkkc" /> * 997 (Chōtoku 3): Provisional Head of the Ōkura Bureau and Provisional Assistant Governor of Harima Province<ref name="mkkc">''Midō Kanpakuki,'' Chōtoku 4.</ref> * 998 (Chōtoku 4): Full Head of the Ōkura Bureau<ref name="gk">''Gonki''.</ref> * 1013 (Chōwa 2): Provisional Assistant Governor of the Right Capital<ref name="syk">''Shōyūki''.</ref> * 1015 (Chōwa 4): Passed away<ref name="syk" />.
== Lineage ==
*Father: {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|qid=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori|1=Kamo no Yasunori}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Daughter of {{ill|Yuge Nakatsuna|ja|弓削仲宣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Nakatsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Morimichi|ja|賀茂守道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Morimichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (986–1030)'''
*Children by unknown mothers: **'''Son: {{ill|Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja|賀茂義行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Yoshiyuki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' == Depictions in Fiction ==
* ''{{ill|Kuwano no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwano no Kizuna|1=Kuon no Kizuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10877708}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|qid=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji (manga)|1=Onmyōji}}'' * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Kyō|ja|陰陽ノ京|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kyō|1=Q11658258|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11658258}}'' * ''Heian Akashiyaka Ayakashi Onmyōji'' * ''Neko Neko Nihonshi'' * ''Kyoto Fushimi no Ayakashi Amamijō''
